title: clarify keys section in polli-cli readme
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
Splits the keys section into secret vs publishable so readers know which to pick, and notes that keys can't be edited (revoke + recreate). Triggered by a user hitting that friction point.

No code changes — README only.

gist.headline: The keys to the hive
gist.blurb: We've polished up the polli-cli documentation to clearly explain the difference between secret and publishable keys. No more guessing which one to use!
gist.summary: Updated the polli-cli README to separate secret and publishable keys and clarify that existing keys must be revoked and recreated rather than edited.
gist.impact: Developers will have an easier time managing their API keys without hitting friction points around editing or choosing the right key type.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee organizing shiny pixel-art keys (one glowing/secret, one standard/publishable) on a cozy wooden desk with a retro manual or book open, representing documentation.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot organizing two shiny pixelated keys—one glowing softly, one standard—on a wooden desk next to an open retro manual. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
